method.input_shape: chapter-N last 3 beats (summary + last_sentence) + chapter-N+1 first 2 beats (summary + first_sentence)
method.calibrationSample: n=10 evenly-distributed rotation pairs across 3 books

cross_book_directional.verdict: 3-way modal across all 3 books: paused (55.1%). Per-book resolved-rate cs=55.56%, ss=35.71%, hg=29.41% (range 26.2pt; diverges). Per-book transitioned-rate cs=5.6%, ss=0.0%, hg=5.9%. Per-book paused-rate cs=38.9%, ss=64.3%, hg=64.7%.

pair_records.reason: The cosmic conflict of Errtu seeking the shard ends with the shard landing in the Realms and Errtu's defeat; chapter 1 shifts to a new interpersonal thread.
Chapter 1's interpersonal conflict among wizards is absent in Chapter 2, which introduces a new character and setting.
Chapter 2's physical conflict with yeti is resolved; chapter 3 cuts to a new setting and characters, pausing the thread.
The interpersonal conflict over leadership is closed with Beorg's declaration and blood-oath ritual.
Chapter 4 ends with Kessell's cosmic conflict resolved (goblin subjugated), but Chapter 5 cuts to Bruenor's unrelated POV, leaving the thread off-stage.
The interpersonal conflict of swaying the council is resolved with Kemp's declaration and the alliance formed.
The external physical conflict of the alliance against a common enemy ends with the alliance dissolving and Drizzt rescued, closing the chapter's conflict.
Wulfgar's internal conflict over his hatred for Bruenor resolves into acceptance and gratitude, closing the chapter's arc.
Kessell and Errtu reach a binding agreement, closing the cosmic conflict over the shard's control.
Bruenor's internal struggle to enchant the hammer is completed successfully; the conflict is closed.
The interpersonal conflict between Bruenor and Wulfgar over the warhammer is closed with the oath and gift, ending the chapter's central tension.
Chapter 13's cosmic conflict (Kessell's rise) is off-stage; chapter 14 cuts to Bruenor/Wulfgar's interpersonal thread.
Chapter 14's interpersonal conflict between Wulfgar and Drizzt is unresolved and off-stage in chapter 15, which shifts to a new external threat.
Errtu is banished, the demonic threat is defeated, and the physical conflict closes.
Chapter 27's interpersonal conflict (Drizzt & Guenhwyvar) is unresolved and off-stage; Chapter 28 cuts to Regis vs. Kessell.
The battle conflict ends with the allied charge, and the epilogue shifts to post-battle looting, indicating closure.
Kessell's cosmic conflict with Crenshinibon morphs from internal temptation to external army-building in the next chapter.
Wulfgar's internal struggle is unresolved and off-stage; epilogue3 cuts to a different thread.
Regis's internal conflict is unresolved and off-stage; chapter 1 cuts to Entreri's POV.
Chapter 3 ends with the group escaping Luskan; Chapter 4 shifts to describing the Hosttower, leaving the physical conflict off-stage.
Chapter 4 ends with Catti-brie's journey to warn friends; Chapter 5 cuts to the other companions, leaving her conflict off-stage.
Chapter 7 ends with reflection on the Hosttower meeting; Chapter 8 cuts to the companions escaping crags, leaving the external physical conflict off-stage.
Chapter 8's interpersonal conflict among companions is absent from Chapter 9's new POV and external threat.
Chapter 10's external physical conflict (Trollmoors danger, Dendybar's scheming) is absent from chapter 11's opening, which cuts to Entreri's party.
Chapter 11 ends with the party in Silverymoon discussing plans; Chapter 12 cuts to Bruenor's group in the Trollmoors, leaving the interpersonal conflict off-stage.
The external physical conflict with trolls ends as companions burst through to safety; chapter 14 shifts to internal aftermath.
Drizzt's internal depression is resolved through Alustriel's intervention, restoring his hope and faith.
The physical trap conflict ends with Drizzt and Entreri swallowed by the cave-in, closing the external threat.
Chapter 20's internal conflict (group tension over greed vs. mission) is off-stage; Chapter 21 cuts to Drizzt's separate physical struggle.
The dragon is defeated and killed, ending the physical combat conflict.
Chapter 24's interpersonal conflict (grieving Bruenor, escaping Duergar) is absent from chapter part1's internal journal entry.
Drizzt's internal conflict over the Gondsman's philosophy concludes with a definitive stance, closing the chapter's driving question.
The internal conflict of Guenhwyvar's anxiety is off-stage in chapter 1, which shifts to Drizzt and Wulfgar's interpersonal debate.
Regis's deception succeeds, the sailor is terrified and flees; the specific interpersonal conflict is closed.
The physical confrontation with Agatha the banshee ends decisively: Drizzt and Wulfgar flee, honoring their promise, and villagers confirm the victory.
Chapter 4 ends with an interpersonal revelation about Bruenor; Chapter 5 cuts to a different POV and setting, leaving that conflict off-stage.
Chapter 6 ends with Entreri's external physical conflict resolved (ship secured), but Chapter 7 cuts to Bruenor's internal scene, leaving Entreri's thread off-stage.
Bruenor's internal conflict is unresolved and off-stage; chapter 8 cuts to Pasha Pook in Calimport.
Chapter 8 ends with LaValle's interior resolve to find Entreri; Chapter 9 cuts to a different POV (Sea Sprite crew) with no mention of that conflict.
Chapter 9 ends with Pook ordering ships to intercept pursuers; Chapter 10 cuts to Bruenor's nightmare and waking, leaving the external physical conflict off-stage.
Chapter 10 ends with the chariot conflict unresolved; Chapter 11 cuts to a different POV and setting, leaving the prior conflict off-stage.
The pirate ship battle ends with Bruenor's boarding and the enemy ship fleeing; the physical conflict is closed.
Chapter 12's interpersonal conflict (Drizzt's suspicion of Pook) is not resolved or reshaped; chapter 13 cuts to a new setting and external physical conflict.
Chapter 15 ends with the group's desert journey unresolved; Chapter 16 cuts to Entreri's POV, leaving the conflict off-stage.
Chapter 16 ends with Drizzt's internal conflict unresolved; Chapter 17 cuts to a different POV and thread.
Pook's interpersonal conflict with Rassiter morphs into the external hunt for the Northerners in chapter 18.
Entreri kills Rassiter, ending the physical confrontation; the specific conflict is closed.
The interpersonal conflict among the companions ends with shared laughter and Drizzt's open declaration, closing the chapter's tension.
The external physical conflict (Drizzt/Wulfgar vs. Entreri's trap) is unresolved and off-stage; epilogue2 cuts to Entreri's POV.
